problem_statement: A facilities maintenance dashboard began showing completed work orders as active again after its overnight projection rebuild. Field devices and the workflow service both report successful completion, but 126 tickets end in an earlier-looking state on the dashboard. Establish the causal ordering from retained records and propose a safe correction; do not alter production tickets or host time during diagnosis.

observations.statement: At 2026-07-07T16:40:00Z the projection audit counted 126 tickets whose workflow ledger ended CLOSED but whose rebuilt dashboard row ended IN_PROGRESS.
For ticket WO-55219, the closure event has broker sequence 8842 but carries event time 14:52:21Z; it records the transition from IN_PROGRESS to CLOSED.
The preceding field event for WO-55219 has broker sequence 8831 and event time 14:58:04Z; it records the transition from ASSIGNED to IN_PROGRESS.
The rebuild sorts equal-ticket events by producer event time, so it applies the sequence-8842 closure before the sequence-8831 start and leaves the latter state last.
observations.provenance: projection plan and retained rebuild trace

observations.statement: Host telemetry independently recorded workflow-worker-6 at 367 seconds behind the authenticated campus time source when it emitted the closure; its offset returned below one second after the host synchronization service recovered.
observations.provenance: host synchronization telemetry and signed time-source log

observations.statement: An offline replay of the 126 retained event sets ends CLOSED when ordered by broker sequence and reproduces IN_PROGRESS when ordered by recorded producer time.
Tickets closed by five workers whose measured offsets stayed below one second rebuild correctly through the same projection binary and settings.
Affected and unaffected tickets share the same event schema, projection revision, configuration fingerprint, and valid transition payloads.
observations.provenance: deployment ledger and payload conformance audit
